title: Orchestration Flow
description: Canonical schema for an orchestration flow manifest. Orchestration flows define the staged sequence in which modules participate, what artifacts are transferred, and what validations govern each handoff. Only orchestration/ owns flow definitions.

schema_version.description: Version of the orchestration-flow schema used by this manifest.

flow_id.description: Unique stable identifier for this orchestration flow.

description.description: Concise statement of what this flow does and why it exists.

status.description: Current status of this flow definition.

stages.description: Ordered list of stages in this flow. Each stage represents a discrete step in the execution sequence.

stage_id.description: Unique identifier for this stage within the flow.

description.description: Optional description of what happens in this stage.

source_module.description: module_id of the module that emits the artifact at this stage.

target_module.description: module_id of the module that receives the artifact at this stage.

artifact_type.description: Canonical artifact type transferred at this stage.

lifecycle_state_at_handoff.description: Required lifecycle state of the artifact when this stage executes.

required_validations.description: Optional list of validation rules that must pass before this stage executes.

optional.description: If true, this stage may be skipped without failing the flow. Defaults to false.

source_modules.description: All module_id values that act as sources (emitters) in this flow.

target_modules.description: All module_id values that act as targets (receivers) in this flow.

artifact_types.description: All canonical artifact type names transferred across this flow.

required_validations.description: Flow-level validation rules that must pass before the flow may execute.

allowed_lifecycle_states.description: Lifecycle states that artifacts may be in when entering or exiting this flow.

notes.description: Optional free-text notes or rationale for this flow definition.
